name: Timor-Leste

outbound_official.note: no official Timor-Leste government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Cooperation (mnec.gov.tl) has a Services/Citizens section but publishes no consolidated list of visa-free/visa-on-arrival/e-visa destinations for Timorese ordinary-passport holders. The government portal (timor-leste.gov.tl), Immigration Service (migracao.gov.tl), and Customs Authority (customs.gov.tl) cover only inbound visa policy. The mnec.gov.tl/node/29 page (Europe relations) mentions the EU visa-waiver (2015) and UK visa exemption for Timorese citizens, but is not a structured outbound visa-list. Checked: mnec.gov.tl, mnec.gov.tl/citizen, mnec.gov.tl/node/29, timor-leste.gov.tl, migracao.gov.tl. No India-equivalent (mea.gov.in/vffin) origin-side page found.

visa_types.name: Tourist Visa / Visa on Arrival (Class I)
visa_types.purpose: Allows entry into Timor-Leste for tourism, family visits, and business prospecting. Available on arrival at Dili International Airport and Dili Sea Port, or in advance at an embassy/consulate.
visa_types.notes: On-arrival grants up to 30 days single entry for USD $30. Advance applications (embassy/consulate) allow up to 90 days with single or multiple entries. Extendable in-country: USD $35 for up to 30 additional days or USD $75 for 30–60 additional days (per customs.gov.tl; migracao.gov.tl states $40 for extension - discrepancy between official pages). Maximum cumulative stay 90 days. Holders may not engage in paid professional activity. Minimum funds: USD $100 per entry plus USD $50 per day. Passport must be valid 6+ months with at least one blank page.

visa_types.name: Transit Visa (VTU)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals passing through Timor-Leste en route to another country, allowing brief ground transit.
visa_types.notes: Maximum 72 hours. Up to 2 entries permitted. Extension is not allowed. Requires travel document, proof of financial means, return/onward ticket, accommodation declaration, and documents justifying transit purpose.

visa_types.name: Airport Transit Visa (VEA)
visa_types.purpose: For travelers who need access to the international zone of the airport and are continuing travel on the same or another aircraft without entering Timor-Leste territory.
visa_types.notes: Maximum 72 hours. Airside transit only - no entry into Timor-Leste territory. Extension not permitted. Requires travel document, proof of financial means, onward ticket, and accommodation declaration.

visa_types.name: Business Visa Class I (VNG1)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals entering Timor-Leste to conduct business prospecting activities or explore investment opportunities.
visa_types.notes: Maximum stay 60 days. Extension is not permitted. Multiple entries allowed. Requires travel document, proof of financial means, return ticket, accommodation declaration, documentation justifying purpose, and professional qualifications proof.

visa_types.name: Business Visa Class II (VNG2)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals settling in Timor-Leste to conduct business as members or administrators of a company registered locally, requiring stays exceeding 183 days per year.
visa_types.notes: Initial duration 6 months. Extendable for 2-year periods at USD $150 per renewal. Multiple entries. Requires certified commercial registration copy, economic activity authorization, tax payment certificate, medical certificate, criminal record from country of origin/residence, and professional qualifications documentation.

visa_types.name: Work Visa (VTB)
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals to enter Timor-Leste to pursue an occupation for others under an employment contract or service agreement, including volunteers working more than 120 days per year.
visa_types.notes: Valid 1 year, renewable for equal periods at USD $100. Processing split: 15 days Immigration Office + 15 days SEFOPE Office. Requires certified employment/service contract, medical certificate, criminal record from country of origin, professional qualifications, employer's commercial registration and tax payment certificate, accommodation declaration, return ticket, and proof of financial support.

visa_types.name: Temporary Stay Visa – Student (VET1)
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals to reside in Timor-Leste for the duration of their study programme at a recognised institution.
visa_types.notes: Duration matches the length of the study period. Extendable in 6-month increments at USD $50. Multiple entries allowed. Requires school declaration, medical certificate, criminal record, proof of accommodation, and proof of financial means.

visa_types.name: Temporary Stay Visa – Specialized Activities (VET2)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals undertaking specialized work, technical missions, or contracted activities in Timor-Leste that do not qualify as standard employment.
visa_types.notes: Valid up to 1 year (or duration of contract/mission). Extendable for equal periods at USD $50. Multiple entries. Requires work contract, professional qualifications, tax certificate, and medical clearance.

visa_types.name: Temporary Stay Visa – Long-Term Volunteer (VET3)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals undertaking long-term volunteer activities in Timor-Leste not exceeding 120 days per year.
visa_types.notes: Maximum 120 days. Extension not available. Multiple entries. Requires volunteer agreement and medical certificate. Volunteers exceeding 120 days annually should apply for the Work Visa (VTB) instead.

visa_types.name: Temporary Stay Visa – Family Dependents (VET4)
visa_types.purpose: For family members who are dependents of a foreign national legally residing in Timor-Leste under a qualifying visa or permit.
visa_types.notes: Duration and extension period match the sponsoring family member's visa/permit. Multiple entries. Requires certified proof of family relationship and medical certificate.

visa_types.name: Temporary Stay Visa – Other Activities (VET5)
visa_types.purpose: Catch-all category for foreign nationals pursuing activities in Timor-Leste not covered by other Temporary Stay Visa sub-types.
visa_types.notes: Duration and conditions determined on a case-by-case basis. Fee is USD $50. Documentation requirements depend on the specific activity.

visa_types.name: Resident Stay Visa – Professional Activities (VFR1)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals intending to establish lawful residence in Timor-Leste for professional purposes, as a precursor to applying for a Temporary Resident Permit.
visa_types.notes: 90 days, multiple entries. Extension not available - holders are expected to apply for a Temporary Resident Permit during this period. Requires professional credentials, commercial registration (certified), employer's tax certificate, medical certificate, and criminal record from country of origin.

visa_types.name: Resident Stay Visa – Family Reunification (VFR2)
visa_types.purpose: For family members of Timor-Leste citizens or legal residents seeking to join them and establish long-term residence through family reunification.
visa_types.notes: 90 days, multiple entries. Extension not available - holders are expected to apply for a Temporary Resident Permit. Requires certified family relationship documentation (consular verification), medical certificate, and criminal record from country of origin.

visa_types.name: Courtesy Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign government officials, diplomats, and other persons entitled to courtesies under Timor-Leste law or international convention.
visa_types.notes: Listed as a distinct visa category by the Timor-Leste Immigration Service. Detailed conditions, fees, and duration are not published on the official website. Issued on a case-by-case basis through diplomatic channels.

visa_free.nationality: Schengen Area (EU member states fully applying Schengen acquis, plus Norway, Iceland, Switzerland, Liechtenstein)
visa_free.notes: Visa-free under EU-Timor-Leste Short-Stay Visa Waiver Agreement signed 26 May 2015 (provisional application from signing; ratified by European Parliament 15 December 2015). Maximum 90 days in any 180-day period. Covers all passport categories (ordinary, diplomatic, service/official, special). Excludes paid employment. UK and Ireland excluded (not part of Schengen area). Norway, Iceland, Switzerland, Liechtenstein covered by associated bilateral declarations on same terms.

Portuguese nationals receive visa exemption for tourism per Immigration Service of Timor-Leste (migracao.gov.tl) and Customs Authority (customs.gov.tl). Also covered by Schengen agreement above. Portuguese nationals (along with US and Indonesian nationals) are exempt from the advance Visa Application Authorization requirement at land border crossings. Duration not specified explicitly on official pages beyond 'visa exemption for tourism'.

US nationals are visa-exempt for tourism per Timor-Leste Customs Authority (customs.gov.tl). US nationals (along with Portuguese and Indonesian nationals) are exempt from the advance Visa Application Authorization requirement at land border crossings. Duration not specified explicitly on official page. Note: migracao.gov.tl/zip/html/sub0301.php does not mention US nationals by name - exemption confirmed only via customs.gov.tl.

Visa-free at all border posts including all land border crossings, Dili International Airport, and Dili Sea Port. Indonesian nationals confirmed as exempt from advance Visa Application Authorization at land borders per customs.gov.tl and migracao.gov.tl. Note: customs.gov.tl describes Indonesian access as on-arrival application privilege at any border, not explicitly 'visa-free'; classification as visa-free follows customs.gov.tl and migracao.gov.tl combined reading. Introduction date (September 19, 2019) cited from aggregators; specific press release URL on timor-leste.gov.tl not independently confirmed. Stay extendable once for same period.

Visa exemption for ordinary passport holders under bilateral agreement signed 21 July 2014. Covers travel for tourism, visits, and business. Agreement announced on Government of Timor-Leste official site. Stay of 30 days, extendable.

Visa waiver for ordinary passport holders only, for tourism purposes. No employment (self or otherwise) permitted. Agreement signed 20-21 June 2024. Timor-Leste National Parliament ratified 2 October 2024 (61 votes in favor). CONFIRMED IN FORCE: Agreement entered into force 22 November 2024, confirmed by Royal Thai Embassy in Dili (dili.thaiembassy.org official notice). Maximum 30 days.

Visa waiver covering Diplomatic, Official/Service, and Ordinary Passport holders. Confirmed by Tatoli report on TL National Parliament ratification 2 October 2024 (61 votes in favor). Agreement signed July 2024; CONFIRMED IN FORCE as of 3 July 2024 per multiple corroborating sources (US-ASEAN Business Council, VisaGuide.News). Maximum 30 days for tourism purposes.

Two separate agreements: (1) Diplomatic/official passports signed October 2024. (2) Ordinary passport holders: agreement for 30-day visa-free travel ratified by TL National Parliament 13 May 2025 per Tatoli. Entry into force for ordinary passports confirmed 15 August 2025 per Laotian Times. 30 days visa-free.

Visa exemption for all passport types (diplomatic, official, service, and ordinary). Council of Ministers approved for parliamentary ratification 29 May 2025 (timor-leste.gov.tl). National Parliament ratified 24 June 2025 (58 votes in favor, 0 against) per Tatoli. CONFIRMED IN FORCE: Joint Statement from Malaysian Prime Minister's official visit confirms visa waiver effective 16 August 2025 (timor-leste.gov.tl ?p=45380). Max stay 30 days inferred from ASEAN-norm bilateral agreements; not explicitly stated on official TL government page.

visa_on_arrival.notes: Visa on arrival available at Dili International Airport and Dili Sea Port only. Fee: USD $30 cash (single entry). Transit visa: USD $20 for stays under 3 days. Extendable in-country: USD $35 for up to 30 additional days or USD $75 for 30-60 additional days (per customs.gov.tl; migracao.gov.tl states $40 for extension - discrepancy between official pages). Maximum cumulative stay 90 days. Travelers must show: sufficient funds (USD $100 per entry + USD $50 per day of stay), valid passport 6+ months with at least one blank page, return/onward ticket, accommodation proof. Land border arrivals (except Indonesians, Portuguese, and US nationals) must obtain advance Visa Application Authorization.

visa_policy.notes: Timor-Leste does not operate an e-visa system as of June 2026. Most visitors obtain a 30-day visa on arrival at Dili Airport or Sea Port for USD $30. EU/Schengen citizens enjoy 90-day visa-free stays under the 2015 EU bilateral agreement. US nationals, Portuguese nationals, and Indonesian nationals are visa-exempt (US and Portuguese confirmed for tourism; Indonesians visa-free at all border posts including land borders). Timor-Leste became the 11th ASEAN member on 26 October 2025. Recent bilateral visa-free agreements now confirmed in force: Thailand (22 Nov 2024), Singapore (3 Jul 2024), Laos ordinary-passport (15 Aug 2025), and Malaysia (16 Aug 2025). All four reflect ASEAN accession-related visa liberalisation. Angola (AGO): TL parliament approved diplomatic/service waiver Dec 2025 - NOT YET IN FORCE for ordinary passports. Vietnam (VNM): ordinary-passport agreement signed June 9 2026 - NOT YET IN FORCE.

conditional_access.conditions: Visa exemption for holders of diplomatic and service passports only. Agreement signed 22 August 2024 (Timor-Leste Foreign Minister and Philippines Secretary of State for Foreign Affairs). Timor-Leste National Parliament ratified the agreement 13 May 2025 per Tatoli search result. Ordinary passport holders are NOT covered.

Visa exemption for single or multiple entry for holders of diplomatic and official passports. Agreement signed 16 December 2024 in Dhaka, witnessed by President Jose Ramos Horta. Signatories: Timor-Leste Foreign Minister Bendito dos Santos Freitas and Bangladesh Home Advisor Md Jahangir Alam Chowdhury. Ordinary passport holders are NOT covered. Sourced from Tatoli (national news agency); no official .gov.tl domain confirmation found.

Visa waiver limited to Diplomatic and Official/Service Passport holders only. Ordinary passport holders are NOT covered (explicitly distinguished from Singapore and Thailand agreements in Tatoli article). Approved by Timor-Leste National Parliament 2 October 2024 (61 votes in favor). Entry into force pending ratification by both parties; no official .gov.tl page confirms dual-ratification status.

Visa exemption for holders of diplomatic and service passports only. Timor-Leste National Parliament unanimously approved the agreement 9 December 2025. Ordinary passport holders are NOT covered. Entry into force date not confirmed on any official .gov.tl page. Sourced from Tatoli (national news agency).

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program exists in Timor-Leste. No official government source was found announcing any such program. The Immigration Service (migracao.gov.tl) offers standard residency permits under Migration Law 11/2017 but no investor citizenship track.

programs.name: Temporary Resident Permit
programs.notes: Valid 2 years, renewable. Eligibility: professional employment, foreign spouses (married 2-5 years to Timorese), family reunification, human trafficking victims, exceptional circumstances. Fee: USD $100 issuance and renewal. Temporary permit holders become eligible for Permanent Residence after 6+ years.

programs.name: Permanent Resident Permit
programs.notes: No expiration. Eligibility: 10+ consecutive years legal residency; OR dependent minor children of nationals; OR spouse of Timorese citizen (5+ years marriage); OR temporary permit holder for 6+ years. Requirements: no serious criminal convictions, demonstrated financial stability and housing, positive economic or social contribution. Fee: USD $100 issuance. Law: Migration Law 11/2017.

agreements: ASEAN Framework Agreement on Visa Exemption (TL parliament ratified 24 February 2026; notification to ASEAN Secretariat pending; inbound reciprocal obligations not yet confirmed in force)

validation_notes: 2026-06-29 ADDED: Angola (AGO) to conditional_access - diplomatic/service passports only, 90 days visa-free. TL National Parliament unanimously approved 9 December 2025 per Tatoli. Ordinary passports NOT covered. Entry into force date unconfirmed. Source: en.tatoli.tl/2025/12/09/timor-leste-parliament-approves-visa-waiver-agreement-with-angola/14/

2026-06-29 UPDATED: Philippines (PHL) in conditional_access - added that TL National Parliament ratified the diplomatic/service agreement on 13 May 2025 per Tatoli search result. Agreement originally signed 22 August 2024. Ordinary passports still NOT covered.

2026-06-29 NOTED: Vietnam (VNM) - ordinary passport visa exemption agreement signed 9 June 2026 during PM Xanana Gusmão's visit to Hanoi. Agreement NOT YET IN FORCE. Added to gaps. Source: vietnamnews.vn/politics-laws/1783085.

2026-06-29 NOTED: ASEAN Framework Agreement on Visa Exemption - TL National Parliament ratified 24 February 2026 (56 votes in favor). Provides 14-day minimum visa-free access among member states. TL government to notify ASEAN Secretariat; entry into force timing unclear. Inbound implications for Timor-Leste not yet confirmed in any official domestic source - added to agreements array as pending.

2026-06-29 CONFIRMED: Laos (LAO) - updated notes to reflect TL parliament ratification date of 13 May 2025 per Tatoli for ordinary passport agreement. Two separate agreements confirmed: diplomatic/official (signed Oct 2024) and ordinary passport (ratified May 2025, in force 15 Aug 2025).

2026-06-29 CONFIRMED: Thailand (THA) - ordinary passports only (explicitly confirmed by Thai Embassy Dili notice). Employment (self or otherwise) prohibited. Entry into force 22 November 2024. No change to classification.
2026-06-29 CONFIRMED: Singapore (SGP) - Tatoli Oct 2024 article confirms ordinary passports ARE covered. Record classification unchanged. source_official remains false (Tatoli, not .gov.tl).

2026-06-29 CONFIRMED: customs.gov.tl re-fetched - still lists Portuguese and US nationals as visa-exempt for tourism. migracao.gov.tl re-fetched - mentions only Portuguese nationals explicitly (not USA). USA classification maintained on basis of customs.gov.tl only.
2026-06-29 CONFIRMED: Malaysia (MYS) - effective 16 August 2025. No change.
2026-06-29 CONFIRMED: Cape Verde (CPV) - 30 days, ordinary passports, tourism/visit/business, 2014 agreement. Re-confirmed via timor-leste.gov.tl. No change.
PREVIOUS VALIDATION NOTES (carried forward):

2026-06-27 VERIFIED: Thailand (THA) visa-free now confirmed in force effective 22 November 2024 per Royal Thai Embassy in Dili official announcement (dili.thaiembassy.org). Previous record noted ratification pending; corrected to reflect confirmed in-force status. Source updated to Thai Embassy Dili official notice.

2026-06-27 VERIFIED: Singapore (SGP) visa-free confirmed in force as of 3 July 2024 per multiple corroborating sources (US-ASEAN Business Council, VisaGuide.News). Previous record noted ratification status unclear; corrected. Notes updated to reflect confirmed in-force status.

2026-06-27 VERIFIED: Malaysia (MYS) effective date now confirmed as 16 August 2025 per joint statement on timor-leste.gov.tl (?p=45380). Previous record lacked an effective date; now confirmed. Max stay 30 days remains as inferred from ASEAN norms (not explicitly stated on official TL page). Source_url updated to joint statement page.

2026-06-27 VERIFIED: USA visa-free remains - customs.gov.tl explicitly names US nationals as visa-exempt for tourism (re-confirmed on this validation run). migracao.gov.tl/zip/html/sub0301.php does not name US nationals explicitly; customs.gov.tl remains the authoritative source.

2026-06-27 VERIFIED: Indonesia - customs.gov.tl describes Indonesian access as on-arrival application privilege at any border post. Combined with migracao.gov.tl this supports visa-free classification. Note added in record clarifying the two-source basis.
2026-06-27 VERIFIED: Portugal visa-free confirmed via both customs.gov.tl and migracao.gov.tl. No change.
2026-06-27 VERIFIED: Laos (LAO) remains in visa_free - timor-leste.gov.tl (?p=40083) confirms ordinary-passport coverage; effective 15 Aug 2025 per Laotian Times. No change to classification.
2026-06-27 VERIFIED: Schengen/EU 90-day visa-free - no change; original source still valid.
2026-06-27 VERIFIED: Brunei (BRN) remains in conditional_access (diplomatic/service/official only, ordinary NOT covered). No change.
2026-06-27 VERIFIED: Cape Verde (CPV) visa-free under 2014 agreement - no change; source timor-leste.gov.tl (?p=10351) remains valid.

ADDED: United States (USA) to visa_free - customs.gov.tl explicitly lists US nationals as visa-exempt for tourism and exempt from land-border Visa Application Authorization. Was absent from previous record despite being in the official source.

MOVED: Laos (LAO) from conditional_access (diplomatic/service only) to visa_free - timor-leste.gov.tl (?p=40083) confirms the Oct 2024 agreement covered ordinary passports; Laotian Times (Sept 2025) confirms entry into force for ordinary passports on 15 August 2025.

ADDED: Malaysia (MYS) to visa_free - new entry. Council of Ministers approved 29 May 2025 (timor-leste.gov.tl) and National Parliament ratified 24 June 2025 (Tatoli) for all passport types including ordinary. Was absent from previous record.
BRUNEI (BRN): Added explicit max_stay_days: 30 inferred from Tatoli article context (same 30-day framework as Singapore/Thailand). Remains in conditional_access as ordinary passports are NOT covered.
VOA EXTENSION FEE: Noted discrepancy - customs.gov.tl lists $35/$75; migracao.gov.tl lists $40 for extension. Both official; discrepancy flagged in visa_on_arrival notes.
timor-leste.be removed from official_domains - the Belgian embassy site is not a TL government domain.
EU agreement note updated: 'every six months' corrected to standard '180-day period' language consistent with official source.
ADDED: visa_types array - 14 visa types extracted from migracao.gov.tl sub0301–sub0308 pages.
gaps: USA visa-free duration not specified on customs.gov.tl or migracao.gov.tl - recorded as null.
Portugal visa-free duration not specified on official pages - recorded as null.
Malaysia max stay duration not specified in joint statement or ratification article; 30 days inferred from ASEAN bilateral norms.

Singapore agreement: primary source is Tatoli (non-.gov.tl); source_official marked false. No official timor-leste.gov.tl page found announcing the Singapore ordinary-passport agreement's effective date.

Thailand agreement: entry-into-force date 22 November 2024 sourced from Thai Embassy in Dili (dili.thaiembassy.org) - a foreign embassy official site; not a timor-leste.gov.tl source. Classified as official.

Vietnam (VNM): ordinary-passport visa exemption agreement signed 9 June 2026 in Hanoi. NOT YET IN FORCE as of 29 June 2026. Entry into force pending ratification by both parliaments. Will need re-check before adding to visa_free. Source: vietnamnews.vn/politics-laws/1783085/viet-nam-timor-leste-exchange-agreement-on-visa-exemption.html

ASEAN Framework Agreement on Visa Exemption (signed 2006, TL acceded Feb 2026): provides 14-day minimum visa-free inbound access among ASEAN member states. TL parliament ratified 24 Feb 2026; TL government to notify ASEAN Secretariat. Inbound reciprocal impact (ASEAN citizens entering TL visa-free for 14 days) unclear - existing bilateral agreements with THA/SGP/MYS/LAO/BRN all exceed 14 days; Cambodia/Myanmar/Vietnam/Philippines/Indonesia coverage under this framework not yet confirmed in any official TL source. Do NOT add ASEAN citizens to visa_free until in-force status confirmed per official TL source.

Philippines (PHL): TL parliament ratified diplomatic/service agreement 13 May 2025 per Tatoli search result. Entry into force date and Philippines-side ratification not confirmed. Ordinary passports still NOT covered.
Angola (AGO): diplomatic/service agreement approved by TL parliament 9 December 2025. Entry into force date not confirmed on any official .gov.tl page.
No official TL government page enumerates a complete structured list of all visa-free/visa-on-arrival nationalities.
No official e-visa portal or system as of June 2026 (confirmed by absence on migracao.gov.tl).
No investor-specific residency or golden visa program found on any official page.

Cambodia, Myanmar, Cuba, China, India: mentioned in aggregators as having diplomatic/service passport visa exemptions but no individual official TL government press release confirmed - omitted per official-source-first rule.
Bangladesh (BGD) diplomatic/official agreement: sourced from Tatoli (national news agency, not .gov domain); no official government ministry confirmation found.
Brunei (BRN) agreement: sourced from Tatoli; no official .gov.tl domain confirmation found. Entry into force not confirmed.
VOA extension fee discrepancy: customs.gov.tl ($35/$75) vs migracao.gov.tl ($40) - both official sources, unresolved.
UN Passport and Interpol Passport holders: Wikipedia states 90-day visa-free entry but no official TL government source confirms this rule.
Indonesia visa-free introduction date (19 September 2019) not confirmed via official timor-leste.gov.tl press release.

Indonesia classification: customs.gov.tl describes as on-arrival application privilege at any border (not explicitly 'visa-free'); visa-free classification rests on combined reading of customs.gov.tl and migracao.gov.tl.
Courtesy Visa: listed as distinct category on migracao.gov.tl but no fee, duration, or eligibility details published on official pages.
No digital nomad, retirement, investment, or working holiday visa categories found on any official Timor-Leste government source.
